critic.verdict: A rhythmic and mostly comfortable chart that occasionally spikes in difficulty with advanced 8th-note patterns and awkward transitions unsuitable for an Easy rating.

issues.problem: Complex 8th-note coordination involving a hold (L -> U Hold -> D Tap) which is too demanding for a beginner-level chart.
Fast 8th-note stream leading directly into a hold (D -> U -> L Hold) that can easily trip up players at this level.
Highly awkward 8th-note sequence (U -> R -> LR Jump) that forces either a very fast double-step on the right foot or an advanced crossover directly into an 8th-note jump entry.
An 8th-note stream (D -> U -> L) that spikes the physical and reading density unnecessarily for an Easy chart.

critic.verdict: A mostly solid Medium chart with good rhythm, but it is severely let down by an impossible note combination and some highly awkward hold-tap patterns.

issues.problem: Requires tapping L while holding both U and D. Since players only have two feet, this is physically impossible to execute cleanly without brackets or hands, which is inappropriate for a Medium chart.
Holding D while tapping a sequence of U, R, L, U, R creates highly awkward crossovers and stretches for a Medium difficulty player.
Holding U while executing 16th-note taps on D and L forces uncomfortable double-steps or unnatural hip twists.

critic.verdict: An exceptionally well-crafted, rhythmic, and fluid hard chart that perfectly captures the energy progression of the song with highly satisfying patterns.

issues.problem: The 8th-note taps (R, L, U) during the D hold require rapid one-foot streaming, which introduces a sudden technical spike compared to the rest of the relatively straightforward intro.
